---
title: "*How Much Entropy Can You Balance on the Head of a Pin (or in an Open Steady-State System)?* Reference Page"
author: "DOFPro group"
format: html
html-math-method: mathjax
---
::: {.callout-important title="Still Under Construction!"}
This web page is just a bare outline. The complete details have not been filled in yet.
:::
# Intro to *How Much Entropy Can You Balance on the Head of a Pin (or in an Open Steady-State System)?*

How Much Entropy Can You Balance on the Head of a Pin (or in an Open Steady-State System)?  explanation

## How Much Entropy Can You Balance on the Head of a Pin (or in an Open Steady-State System)?

Video 1 explanation

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/QcZTezMtDRk?si=MGD6zoSZ6AMhT6-E" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen></iframe>
[Visuals](../../NotYetHere.qmd)

# Examples and Definitions

## Examples Links

## Definitions

[Transient System](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Transient_state)
: The values of process variables are changing with time.

[Steady State System](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Steady_state)
: The values of process variables do not change with time but may change with position. (What EE’s call a DC steady state)

[Closed or Batch System](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Closed_system)
: Throw stuff in. Wait. Pull products out.

[Open or Continuous System](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Open_system_(systems_theory))
: Continually throw stuff in and pull stuff out.


# Other Links and Videos

## Other Links

## Previous and Following Videos

### The video previous to the previous video (i.e., two videos back)

**Sadi Carnot and the Power of Fire, the 1824 Edition Part 1**<br/>*The Air-Standard Carnot Cycle*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/EDJQAvBIAok), [Info Page](../../balances/entropy/TheCarnotCycleVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../balances/entropy/116-Visuals.qmd)

### The previous video (i.e., one video back)

**Sadi Carnot and the Power of Fire, the 1824 Edition Part 2**<br/>*The Steam Carnot Cycle*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/Qc9W1KKpouU), [Info Page](../../balances/entropy/TheCarnotCycleVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../balances/entropy/116.5-Visuals.qmd)

### The next video (i.e., one video forward)

**Entropy Made Me Do It: Turbines, Compressors, and Other Second-Law Shenanigans**<br/>*Turbines and Expanders, Isentropic and Isothermal Compressors*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/Pb7PS5oeCz0), [Info Page](../../balances/entropy/EntropyMadeMeDoItVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../balances/entropy/118-TurbCompEtcVisuals.qmd)

### The next after next video (i.e. two videos forward)

**Entropy Made Me Do It: Pumps, Nozzles, and Other Second-Law Shenanigans**<br/>*Pumps, Nozzles, and Valves or Throttles*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/irBfMfVUEFo), [Info Page](../../balances/entropy/EntropyMadeMeDoItVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../balances/entropy/118.5-PumpNozzVisuals.qmd)
